recent_dislikes.title: Wind and truth
recent_dislikes.author: Brandon Sanderson
recent_dislikes.excerpt: Enjoyable conclusion without the moral weight and grace of the first few books. The twist wasn’t quite twisty enough however a good tale. The world building was incredible. Will read the next story ark.

recent_dislikes.title: Too like the lightning
recent_dislikes.author: Ada Palmer
recent_dislikes.excerpt: Not sure what to make of this one. Challenging. At times sound of hard to follow and the narrator style difficult to follow. The different factions “Hives” hard to keep straight. Not sure what the Bash system adds to the narrative. They are families. Extended families.

recent_dislikes.title: The well of ascension

reviews.excerpt: A glittering tragedy of love and illusion — dazzling to the eye, but cold to the touch. Evocative and beautifully written. At first, I was struck by the contradictions: glorious descriptions of food set against social judgment, self judgment, and the suffocating weight of gender roles. Yuzuki writes entirely from a woman’s perspective, and the

reviews.title: All Systems Red: The Murderbot Diaries
reviews.author: Martha Wells
reviews.excerpt: A very short novel — I finished it in about two days. I loved the premise: a robot that manages to break free of its controls, but chooses not to rebel directly or violently against its masters. reviews.title: The Bonehunters
reviews.author: Steven Erikson
reviews.excerpt: Admired more than I loved. The story didn’t grip me quite as tightly as other Malazan entries, but the moral complexity, thematic richness, and depth of character development made this one of the most intellectually satisfying in the series so far. Overall Impression The sixth Malazan Book of the Fallen novel combines

reviews.author: Steven Erikson
reviews.excerpt: I was captivated by the adventure and again found myself enthralled by the characters and the world Erikson has woven. reviews.title: Ready Player One
reviews.excerpt: The book’s three part structure worked well, each section mirroring the quest stages within the OASIS and Wade’s experiences in the real world. reviews.author: Arkady Martine
reviews.excerpt: This novel explores individuality and personhood with greater depth than its predecessor, especially through Mahit’s evolving relationships with both Yskandr and Three Seagrass. reviews.title: Midnight Tides
reviews.author: Steven Erikson
reviews.excerpt: I was initially disappointed by the absence of the Bridgeburners and other familiar faces — Erikson's abrupt shift to an entirely new continent and cast felt like a gamble. reviews.title: House of chains
reviews.author: Steven Erikson
reviews.excerpt: Another enjoyable read from Erikson. Yet again I was impressed by the scope and scale of his tale. His building of atmosphere was very strong. reviews.title: Memories of ice
reviews.author: Steven Erickson
reviews.excerpt: I can see why people are so passionate about the Malian book of the fallen. The world building is exquisite. Each place felt real. reviews.title: The fifth season
reviews.author: N.K. Jemisin
reviews.excerpt: This was a Fantasy novel of surprising complexity. It was only at the end that I realised that the three lead female chracters were the same person (Damaya) at diffierent times. This created a really full and in depth understanding of her characterisation. The enslavement of the Rogas was a very good theme. Jemisin's t

reviews.title: The children of the mind
reviews.author: Orson Scott Card
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable read but lost some of the philosophical grounding of the first three novels. Still a great adventure story.

reviews.title: The Obelisk Gate
reviews.author: N.K. Jemisin
reviews.excerpt: This very much felt fluke a second book in the trilogy. More characters are introduced and the plot widens out. Our characters start to move independently and some threads start to come together. Jemisin has mastered her use of the present tense third person or I am just getting used to it. The narrative structure was

reviews.title: Last argument of kings
reviews.author: Joe Abercrombie
reviews.excerpt: Delightfully dark. No character is good or evil. Some are just more evil than others. The characters are delightfully drawn with perhaps the exception of Black Dow. I look forward to reading more books by Joe Abercrombie.

reviews.title: Ender in Exile
reviews.author: Orson Scott Card
reviews.excerpt: I was very disappointed by this novel. I did not feel, in context of the rest of the Ender books, that this story needed telling, or at least not in the way it was. The story was pre occupied with a power dynamic between the captain of the colony ship and Ender. The wider development of Ender was not made clear. The re

reviews.title: Best served cold
reviews.author: Joe Abercrombie
reviews.excerpt: I expected another deeply dark tale from Abercrombie and this was delivered. I wasn't sure what to expect. Revenge was the clear driving force through the book and I found this motivation for the characters a little shallow. And it made the book feel a bit like a formulaic heist story. Gather a crew, go through a serie

reviews.title: Bands of mourning
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable great adventure. Felt the characters were well drawn however it was a little predictable and felt a bit hackneyed. But the world is amazing and the lore impressive.

reviews.title: A boy's own story
reviews.author: Edmund White
reviews.excerpt: This was a deeply moving novel. I saw myself reflected in the the protagonist clearly. I found the timeline in the story interesting. It jumped around quite a bit reflecting back and forward in time. White in his post script puts this style down to his drinking at the time of writting. I found it added to the story and

reviews.title: Wind and truth
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable conclusion without the moral weight and grace of the first few books. The twist wasn’t quite twisty enough however a good tale. The world building was incredible. Will read the next story ark.

reviews.title: The rise of Endymion
reviews.author: Dan Simmons
reviews.excerpt: Extremely good. I guessed that Raul would be the father. Don’t see A. Bettick as the observer. Excellent resolution to the story. Mixes metaphysics and theology beautifully. A hymn to humanism and the hope of a better future.

reviews.title: The blade itself
reviews.author: Joe Abercrombie
reviews.excerpt: Extremely enjoyable. Dark bloody but full of humor. The characters and world are vivid and the plot interesting. This really was a great opening.

reviews.title: Speaker for the dead
reviews.author: Orson Scott Card
reviews.excerpt: Interesting follow up to Enders Game. Explores morality and a good character study. How Ended became so unbelievably good at reading people isn’t clear. Liked the description of a completely different life cycle and what that means for morality.

reviews.title: Endymion
reviews.author: Dan Simmons
reviews.excerpt: I loved this book. I struggled with the first two Hyperion books but this one was a joy to read. Mysterious and well paced. The story had me hooked. I look forward to reading the next!

reviews.title: Edge Dancer
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable little novel. Lift is a great character and so enjoyable to read.

reviews.title: Dawnshard
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable short book expanding the universe and lore of the Stormlight Archive series.

reviews.title: Before they are hanged
reviews.author: Joe Abercrombie
reviews.excerpt: Amazing how Abercrombie makes a torturer a sympathetic character. Another thoroughly good read. Incredible character work a slow but building plot. Where is it all going to go.

reviews.title: Ultra-processed people
reviews.author: Chris van Tulleken
reviews.excerpt: bookclub Very readable. Point is very labored at times. Made me reconsider how I eat.

reviews.title: Too like the lightning
reviews.author: Ada Palmer
reviews.excerpt: Not sure what to make of this one. Challenging. At times sound of hard to follow and the narrator style difficult to follow. The different factions “Hives” hard to keep straight. Not sure what the Bash system adds to the narrative. They are families. Extended families.

reviews.title: The alloy of Law
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable return to the world of Mistborn. I enjoy the Victorian line works and the new characters. Tight not group and close focus on three characters.

reviews.title: The Darkness That Comes Before
reviews.author: R. Scott Bakker
reviews.excerpt: Did not finish Couldn’t get into this. Gave up. I found the names confusing and the lore hard to follow. Couldn’t get a feel for the characters or the world.

reviews.title: Shadows of self
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable adventure. More of the comers opening up. Nothing ground breaking on the previous novels.

reviews.title: Prince of thorns
reviews.author: Mark Lawrence
reviews.excerpt: Very enjoyable. Dark and twisted and visceral. You can hear and smell the clash and stink of battle. The stench of the brothers leaps off the page. Pacing excellent descriptions vividly drawn. Questions on the origin of the world. Our world a 1,000 years after nuclear war?

reviews.title: Pandora’s star
reviews.author: Peter F. Hamilton
reviews.excerpt: The first part of the story. We are left with a literal cliff hanger as Ozzie, Orion and their alien companion fall of the edge of a disk world and the prime invasion os happening and key players are realizing the starter os real. The only complaint with the star flyer idea is that it has to be real to be included in t

reviews.title: Judas unchained
reviews.author: Peter F. Hamilton
reviews.excerpt: So the SI wasn’t the starflyer! Enjoyed a lot. I found the Silken and Izzie subplot unnecessary it didn’t carry the story forward and had only a small point towards the plot. Ozzie has humanities conscience could have got in to the plot elsewhere. I enjoyed the style and the epic world building. However the characteriz

reviews.title: Enders game
reviews.author: Orson Scott Card
reviews.excerpt: One of the best books I have read in a very long time. The twist at the end was I suppose, in hindsight, inevitable but I did t see it. Ended is so beautifully drawn. The battle school so well imagined. The story felt vast even though it was short and set in a very small space.

reviews.title: Wrath
reviews.author: John Gwynne
reviews.excerpt: Good if a little predictable. Seemed a little random who Gwynne chose to live and to die. But also there were predictable events. That Gar would die, that Asroth would come into the world. That Corban would reject power. Enjoyed. Will read more from this author.

reviews.title: The well of ascension
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Entertaining. Good twist at the end. Good pace. Will likely read the next one.

reviews.title: The hero of ages
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Reverent twist at the end of full of action, drama and pathos. Though I didn’t engage with the characters as much as I have with other books.

reviews.title: Ruin
reviews.author: John Gwynne
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable read. Fast paced with lots of action. Though the pacing makes the character work less strong and the world building not as good as other novels. Almost every scene is a battle or clash. No quiet moments at all.

reviews.title: Project Hail Mary
reviews.author: Andy Weir
reviews.excerpt: Uplifting exciting and optimistic. A brilliant and engaging story of shared humanity and compassion. I enjoyed the first person style.

reviews.title: Disquiet Gods
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: Greatly enjoyed. The world (universe) building is fantastic. The terror and horror of Vorgossos feels very real. The Christian theology comes over very strongly. Feels a bit heavy handed. The hybrid of fantasy and sci fi works really well.

reviews.title: Valour
reviews.author: John Gwynne
reviews.excerpt: Hugely enjoyable. Fast paced and great characterization. There is a good exploration of characters. They are not all good or all bad. Motivations explored. Clearly influenced by Tolkien and Robert Jordan. But original.

reviews.title: The sunlit man
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable sci fi fantasy fusion.

reviews.title: The final empire
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Starts like a heist story as a crew are brought together but expands to be so much more. Who is this immortal “Lord Ruler” Highly enjoyable. Not as good as the storm light archive books but the adventure and characterization was good. I did find some of the characters a little shallow. Ellen’s could have been deepened.

reviews.title: Malice
reviews.author: John Gwynne
reviews.excerpt: Really enjoyed this. Fast paced and vivid characters. Loved the animal companion. Lines the story telling from different perspectives. Found myself more drawn into Corban’s plot than others and caring about him more. Reminded me of how Robert Jordan used different character perspectives in the wheel of time.

reviews.title: Lessons on Chemistry
reviews.author: Bonnie Garmus
reviews.excerpt: bookclub Enjoyable family saga. Elizabeth Zott the protagonist did not feel real. She is too perfect. She represents the ideal modern female. Independent and courageous. Book gives a good view of mid century sexism and the challenges women face. I found the portrait of Zott as too perfect almost decision itself. She ne

reviews.title: Yellowface
reviews.author: Rebecca F Kuang
reviews.excerpt: bookclub This was a challenging read. It felt like an extended anxiety nightmare. The protagonist is entirely unlikeable. At points I found myself rooting for her if only so her pain would end. Her predicament is entirely self inflicted she makes a series of horrible judgements and entirely selfish choices justified by

reviews.title: The prisoner of heaven
reviews.author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
reviews.excerpt: Another enigmatic novel by Zafón I don’t really know what to make of it except incredibly atmospheric gives a real view of Franco Spain and Barcelona of the 30s 40s and 50s. The plot is dark but simple Daniel unsure of who his father really is learn that his father is not his father or maybe Heiz we learn more about Ma

reviews.title: Red Rising
reviews.author: Pierce Brown
reviews.excerpt: Real page turner. I’m enjoying the universe that is being built. Very much a coming of age story.

reviews.title: Morning star
reviews.author: Pierce Brown
reviews.excerpt: Third in the story. I grew frustrated towards the end that the same cycle of capture and defeat would continue. Brown had me deceived. Loved the twist at the end. Don’t think I will rush to read the next volume. But will get to them I’m sure.

reviews.title: Golden Son
reviews.author: Pierce Brown
reviews.excerpt: Expands on the u inverse of the finally irst novel. We see Darrow grow up a bit and struggle through a number of challenges. Very entertaining and couldn’t put it down. I wounded if they revealed Darrow’s secret of his “Red” origins too soon. Will have to wait to find out.

reviews.title: Dissolution
reviews.author: C.J. Sansom
reviews.excerpt: Very enjoyable read. Real page turner and well researched. Look forward to reading another.

reviews.title: Dark Fire
reviews.author: C.J. Sansom
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyable historical thriller. This novel lacked the dark menace of the first. But was still an enjoyable page turner.

reviews.title: Shōgun
reviews.author: James Clavell
reviews.excerpt: Vivid recreation of late medieval (Elizabethan Japan) the characters well drawn. The story engaging. Sometimes find it difficult to follow the change in perspective as the story switches point of view between characters.

reviews.title: Rythym of War
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Hugely enjoyable with a great deal of character and plot development. Aeolian still feels very 1 dimensional to me. All other characters have failings, flaws but Adolin to perfect. Was a bit too long.

reviews.title: Kingdoms of death
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: Horror pain adventure loss this was a dark but violently told tale. Where this goes in the story or why this needed to happen I do not know but I enjoyed the journey!

reviews.title: Ashes of Man
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: Wow. Read this in one weekend. Fantastic space opera. Fast paced and full of pathos. At times I was close to tears. My pulse high as I read the action. Great fun. But what to read next.

reviews.title: Words of radiance
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Really enjoyed this second installment of the Stormlight archive. The characters are starting to come together. However I felt the ending scenes were a little cheesy. Especially between Dalinar & Shallan. The nature of the universe is starting to come clear.

reviews.title: The way of kings
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Massively enjoyed this fantasy novel. Characters are well drawn and it keeps you guessing. So many cliffhangers and questions at the end. The magic isn’t so well drawn and the nature of storm light feels like it needs some more explanation. I look forward to reading the next one!

reviews.title: The demon in white
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: Another romping adventure. The tale is linking fantasy and sci fi together well. The battle the threat all great. This one feels like a middle book which I suppose it is!

reviews.title: Oathbringer
reviews.author: Brandon Sanderson
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyed this one. Same wonderful battle scenes. But some issues with the pacing and plot. How did they get the singers out of Urithu? Looking forward to the final two books

reviews.title: Howling Dark
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: Really enjoyed this book the second in the sun eater series. The familiar set of characters is here with an expanded set. The threat of Vogossos, the terror of battle and the conflict behind Hadrian’s actions are all really well drawn. Excited to read the next one.

reviews.title: The secret speech
reviews.author: Tom Rob Smith
reviews.excerpt: Another great thriller by Tom Rob Smith. Great romp, lots of blood and gore. The plot stretched credulity but then they do say “stranger then Fiction”. The story continues to follow Leo & Raisa as they try to build their lives with their adopted daughters in post Stalin USSR.

reviews.title: The drawing of the three
reviews.author: Stephen King
reviews.excerpt: Very much enjoyed. Not like a fantasy I’ve read before. Roland, Eddie and Odetta/Detta/Samantha are a great combination. The interplay between them masterfully drawn. The horror real.

reviews.title: The Gun Slinger
reviews.author: Stephen King
reviews.excerpt: Very much tell this was written by a 19 year old. The book felt like one long introductory chapter which I guess it is. So many unanswered questions about this world. Looking forward to reading more.

reviews.title: Surface detail
reviews.author: Ian M Banks
reviews.excerpt: Penultimate culture series novel and this sees the culture at its best. Lots of exciting politics, space battles and mischievous minds all working together at cross purposes with the humans getting caught in between.

reviews.title: Empire of Silence
reviews.author: Christopher Ruocchio
reviews.excerpt: The world is heavily influenced by Dune. With an emperor, noble class, monopolies guilds, a ban on AI with trained humans filling the place of computers. However the universe is richer and the story more relatable. Some of the technology is inspired by “the culture” series. Eg. neural nets. I look forward to reading th

reviews.title: Alien Clay
reviews.author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
reviews.excerpt: Very entertaining but formulaic. The commandant was a one dimensional antagonist.

reviews.title: Hyperion Cantos book 2 the fall of Hyperion
reviews.author: Dan Simmons
reviews.excerpt: Overly long and mystical. Enjoyable in parts. Might consider reading more of the series.

reviews.title: Child 44
reviews.author: Tom Rob Smith
reviews.excerpt: Great adventure and detective model set in Stalin’s Russia where there can be no crime.

reviews.title: Ice people
reviews.author: Tom Rob Smith
reviews.excerpt: Cinematic. Begging for a sequel. Clearly inspired by the three body problem

reviews.title: Hyperion Cantos Book 1
reviews.author: Daniel Simmons
reviews.excerpt: Hard SiFi but interesting. Book clearly the first in a series. Who / what is the Shrike. What is the AI core up to?

reviews.title: City of Thieves
reviews.author: David Benioff
reviews.excerpt: Enjoyed this but very dark. Some of the characters lacked depth. But a great grim adventure

reviews.title: The Angel's Game
reviews.author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
reviews.excerpt: Psychological thriller with ambiguous ending. Second in the graveyard of forgotten books series.

reviews.title: Reasons Not to Worry: How to be Stoic in chaotic times
reviews.author: Brigid Delaney
reviews.excerpt: A very good and practical guide to stoicism. I didn’t like some of the writing style. Referring to “vibes”. Makes me want to read more on the subject.

reviews.title: Great expectations
reviews.author: Charles Dickens
reviews.excerpt: Never read Dickens before. Very good story told from the fist person perspective. Didn’t expect the ending to be quite so melancholy. Dickens created larger than life characters they jump from the page. Some ridiculous. Not sure if they are meant to be believed or are characters.
